zh.lifeSummary: 當Llywelyn ap Leuan ap Llewelyn Fychan於大約1470年出生在威爾士蒙格莫利夏爾利朗利時，他的父親Llywelyn "Fychan" ab Ieuan37歲，母親Jonet ferch Dafydd-30歲。他於大約1498年與Annes ferch Rhys在威爾士結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Llywelyn ap Leuan ap Llewelyn Fychan于大约1470年出生在威尔士蒙格莫利夏爾利朗利时，他的父亲Llywelyn "Fychan" ab Ieuan37岁，母亲Jonet ferch Dafydd-30岁。他于大约1498年与Annes ferch Rhys在威尔士结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Llewelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Llewellyn
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

nameMeanings.name: Fychan
meanings: Welsh: A Welsh name of great antiquity, from vyckan, little, small; i.e. small in stature. Compare the surname Bychan and the Armoric Le Bihan.

nameMeanings.name: Llywelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Llewelyn
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Leuan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Llywelyn ap Leuan ap Llewelyn Fychan was born about 1470, in Llangurig, Montgomeryshire, Wales, his father, Llywelyn "Fychan" ab Ieuan, was 37 and his mother, Jonet ferch Dafydd, was -30. He married Annes ferch Rhys about 1498, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter.

ja.lifeSummary: Llywelyn ap Leuan ap Llewelyn Fychanが約1470年にウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアラングリグで生まれた時，父Llywelyn "Fychan" ab Ieuanは37歳，母Jonet ferch Dafyddは-30歳であった。約1498年にウェールズでAnnes ferch Rhysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Llywelyn ap Leuan ap Llewelyn Fychan(이)가 대략 1470년 웨일스 몽고머이실르 랭리그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Llywelyn "Fychan" ab Ieuan(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Jonet ferch Dafydd(은)는 -30세였다. 그는 대략 1498년 웨일스에서 Annes ferch Rhys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Llywelyn ap Leuan ap Llewelyn Fychan родился около 1470 г. в местности ллангуриг, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Llywelyn "Fychan" ab Ieuan было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Jonet ferch Dafydd, -30. Он женился на женщине по имени Annes ferch Rhys около 1498 г. в местности Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Llywelyn ap Leuan ap Llewelyn Fychan nació aproximadamente 1470, en Llangurig, Montgomeryshire, Gales, su papá, Llywelyn "Fychan" ab Ieuan, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Jonet ferch Dafydd, tenía -30 años. Se casó con Annes ferch Rhys aproximadamente 1498, en Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija.
